title: K8s Pod Details & Scheduler Metrics
description: Rich pod information: phases, errors, container status, scheduling latency, API server health, etcd backend. All filtered by namespace.

panels.title: Running Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Running phase

panels.title: Pending Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Pending phase

panels.title: Succeeded Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Succeeded phase

panels.title: Failed Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Failed phase

panels.title: Unknown Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Unknown phase

panels.title: Pods by Phase Over Time
panels.description: One query, split by phase label. Shows the distribution of all pod phases over time. Each color = a phase (Running, Pending, Succeeded, Failed, Unknown).

panels.title: All Pods — Name, Namespace, Start Time, Uptime, Restarts
panels.description: Every pod with rich details. Start time is in ISO format (kube_pod_start_time * 1000). Restarts = total container restart count. Sorted by most recently started.

panels.title: Containers Waiting (by Reason)
panels.description: Containers in Waiting state. Common reasons: CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, ErrImagePull, CreateContainerConfigError. Each line = a reason.

panels.title: Containers Terminated (by Reason)
panels.description: Containers that were terminated. Reasons: OOMKilled, Error, Completed, ContainerCannotRun, DeadlineExceeded. Spikes indicate problems.

panels.title: Pods with Container Errors — Waiting Reasons
panels.description: Pods that have containers currently in Waiting state. Shows the reason. Filter to see which specific pods are stuck and why.

panels.title: Pods — Last Terminated Reason
panels.description: Shows the most recent termination reason for each container. OOMKilled means the container ran out of memory. Error means it exited non-zero.

panels.title: Container Restarts per Pod Over Time
panels.description: Total restart count per pod. A continuously rising line = crash loop. Flat line = stable. Each line = a pod.

panels.title: Container Restart Rate per Pod
panels.description: Rate of restarts (restarts/sec). Spikes indicate crash loops or OOMKills. Sustained rate > 0 = pod is cycling.

panels.title: Pod Status Reasons (Evicted, NodeLost, etc.)
panels.description: Pods with a status reason set. Evicted = pod was evicted (disk pressure, memory pressure). NodeLost = node became unreachable.

panels.title: Pods Not Ready Over Time
panels.description: Count of pods where readiness probe is failing. These pods exist but are not receiving traffic through Services.

panels.title: Pod Scheduling — Latency, Attempts, Queue Depth
Scheduler Attempts (by Result)
panels.description: How many scheduling attempts per second and whether they succeeded. result=scheduled: pod was placed. result=unschedulable: no node fits. result=error: scheduler internal error.

panels.title: Pods Pending in Scheduler Queue
panels.description: Number of pods waiting in the scheduler queue. queue=unschedulable: pods that failed and are waiting for retry. queue=backoff: pods in exponential backoff. queue=active: pods ready for scheduling attempt.

panels.title: End-to-End Scheduling Latency (p50, p90, p99)
panels.description: Time from pod entering the scheduler queue to being bound to a node. p99 > 1s indicates scheduler is struggling. High latency = not enough resources or complex scheduling constraints.

panels.title: Scheduling Algorithm Duration (p50, p90, p99)
panels.description: Time the scheduling algorithm itself takes (filtering + scoring nodes). Does not include queue wait time. High values = too many nodes or complex predicates.

panels.title: Pods Scheduled vs Unschedulable (rate)
panels.description: Rate at which pods are being successfully scheduled vs failing. A rising unschedulable rate means the cluster is running out of capacity.

panels.title: Scheduler Preemption Attempts
panels.description: Rate of preemption attempts. Preemption = evicting lower-priority pods to make room for higher-priority ones. Frequent preemption indicates resource pressure.

panels.title: Scheduler Plugin Duration by Extension Point
panels.description: Time each scheduler plugin takes at each extension point (Filter, Score, PreFilter, etc.). Helps identify slow scheduler plugins.

panels.title: Unschedulable Pods Count
panels.description: Pods that cannot be scheduled. kube_pod_status_unschedulable shows pods whose PodScheduled condition is False. These are stuck in Pending.

panels.title: Incoming Pods to Scheduler Queue
panels.description: Rate of pods entering the scheduler queue. Spikes = large-scale deployments or scaling events.

panels.title: Currently Unscheduled Pods
panels.description: Pods where PodScheduled condition is False. These are in Pending state waiting for a node. Check Events in kubectl for the specific reason.

panels.title: API Server Request Rate (by verb)
panels.description: Rate of API requests by HTTP verb (GET, LIST, WATCH, CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, PATCH). High LIST/WATCH rates are normal. Spikes in CREATE/DELETE = deployment activity.

panels.title: API Server Errors (4xx, 5xx)
panels.description: Rate of API errors by response code. 409=conflict (normal during updates), 429=throttled, 500+=server errors (bad). Filter: only error codes shown.

panels.title: API Server Request Latency (p50, p90, p99)
panels.description: Time the API server takes to respond. p99 > 1s = overloaded API server or etcd. Verbs like LIST are naturally slower than GET.

panels.title: API Server Inflight Requests
panels.description: Currently in-flight requests to the API server. mutating = write operations, readOnly = read operations. High inflight = API server under pressure.

panels.title: API Latency p99 by Resource (top slow resources)
panels.description: Which Kubernetes resources are slowest to query. Helps identify if pods, nodes, secrets, or configmaps are causing API slowdowns.

panels.title: etcd Request Latency (p99)
panels.description: Latency of API server requests to etcd. etcd slowness directly impacts scheduling and all cluster operations. p99 > 100ms = etcd issues.

panels.title: etcd Object Count by Resource
panels.description: Number of objects stored in etcd by resource type. Helps understand cluster scale.
